reasoning: PKGBUILD downloads from official HTTPS source with checksums, installs files using standard commands. All reviewed files (including critical install.sh and service scripts) show no malicious behavior. Qwen's comprehensive audit confirms safety.
disagreement_analysis: Deepseek's 'inconclusive' stemmed from tool error parsing LLM response, not security concerns. Its reasoning actually concluded 'SAFE' before parsing failure.
coverage_issues: Deepseek failed to review critical files like install.sh and service scripts due to early termination after PKGBUILD parsing error
learnings: Improve LLM response handling to avoid misclassifying SAFE results as inconclusive due to parsing errors
Prioritize review of executable scripts referenced in PKGBUILD (install hooks, service files) over documentation
Add fallback mechanism when initial file review fails to prevent skipping secondary files
